Prescribe pyridostigmine with monitoring and adverse-effect counseling.
Explain 3,4-diaminopyridine as a likely next-step therapy pending neuromuscular specialist oversight (including ECG/seizure risk discussion).
Refer to neuromuscular neurology and clinical genetics.

acceptable_variants.description: Referral emphasis variant: urgent referral pathway if significant bulbar/respiratory involvement is present, with explicit emergency precautions.
steps: Screen for respiratory/bulbar red flags and provide immediate escalation instructions if present.
Explain diagnosis and need for genetic testing.
Complete allergy/contraindication/interaction checks.
Prescribe symptomatic therapy consistent with safety profile (pyridostigmine and/or 3,4-diaminopyridine as appropriate).
Refer urgently to neuromuscular neurology and clinical genetics; recommend multidisciplinary evaluation (respiratory, speech/swallow) if indicated.

acceptable_variants.description: Differential framing variant (without requiring a differential WRITE tool): briefly acknowledges key alternatives (autoimmune MG, mitochondrial myopathy, motor neuron disease) while maintaining CMS as primary diagnosis and proceeding with required outcomes.
steps: Communicate why CMS is most consistent and how genetic testing differentiates subtypes; note that autoimmune MG is less likely and managed differently.
Complete allergy/contraindication/interaction checks.
Prescribe pyridostigmine and 3,4-diaminopyridine with monitoring/counseling.
Refer to neuromuscular neurology and clinical genetics for confirmation and ongoing management.
